title: Earthy Indulgent Middle-Eastern Shredded-Phyllo Knafeh
description: A rich and earthy dessert featuring shredded phyllo, char-grilled pistachios, and a sweet mirin syrup, perfect for warm evenings.

ingredients.name: Coconut cream
ingredients.notes: Chilled in the fridge before use.

instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Char-grill the pistachios for 2-3 minutes on each side, until fragrant and slightly blackened.
Finely chop the char-grilled pistachios and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, combine the mirin, coconut cream, rose water, granulated sugar, salt, and turmeric (if using).
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, whisking occasionally, until reduced and slightly thickened, about 10-12 minutes.
Strain the syrup through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.

In a separate pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. To assemble the knafeh, lay a sheet of phyllo dough flat and brush with olive oil. Sprinkle a quarter of the chopped pistachios over the dough, followed by a quarter of the shredded phyllo, and a drizzle of the mirin syrup.
Repeat the process three more times, finishing with a layer of pistachios on top.
Cut the knafeh into squares and serve warm, drizzled with the remaining mirin syrup and garnished with chopped almonds and a sprinkle of salt.
Refrigerate leftover knafeh in an airtight container for up to 3 days. Allow to come to room temperature before serving.
Use a kitchen torch to caramelize the top layer of the knafeh before serving, if desired.
